case.focal_claim: ... [the study] finds substantially higher income for low-caste households residing in villages dominated by BACs [compared to villages dominated by upper caste].

original_datasets.name: household

summary_statistics.info: 1666 rows x 85 columns; dtypes: category(5), float32(66), float64(10), int16(2), int32(2). Key columns: hhcode (int16), village (float32), caste (category: ST/SC, Back agr, Back oth), totinc (float32).

original_datasets.notes: Household-level data. village is numeric in source and needs casting before merge. Used to obtain totinc and caste.

original_datasets.name: village

summary_statistics.info: 122 rows x 30 columns; domhigh non-null for 90 villages; dtypes: float32(16), float64(14).

original_datasets.notes: Village-level data; domhigh indicates high-caste dominated (1) vs low-caste dominated (0). Filter to domhigh in {0,1} before analysis.

tasks.task_instruction: Conduct the analysis without any restrictions.

analysis_path.path_description: Merge household and village data; fit a linear mixed model predicting total income from caste, village dominance (domhigh), and their interaction with a random intercept for village.
preprocessing_steps: Cast village IDs to string for consistent merging.
Select hhcode, village, caste, totinc from household.
Merge domhigh from village onto household by village.
Filter to observations with domhigh in {0,1}.

key_choices.main_predictor_measure: Village dominance indicator (domhigh: 1=high-caste dominated, 0=low-caste dominated) with caste categories and their interaction.
key_choices.sample_restriction: Keep households in villages with domhigh coded 0 or 1.
key_choices.missing_data_rule: Implicit listwise deletion via filtering domhigh to {0,1}.
key_choices.data_processing: Type casting of keys; left join household to village; restrict to needed variables.
key_choices.model_choice: Linear mixed-effects model with random intercept for village.
key_choices.inference_rule: Two-sided p-values for fixed effects at alpha=0.05.

main_predictor.name: domhigh (with caste and interaction)
main_predictor.definition: Indicator for high-caste dominated village (1) vs low-caste dominated village (0), interacted with household caste categories.

analysis_code.notes: Python translation of the provided R (lmer) analysis. Uses statsmodels MixedLM. Expects data under /app/data/AEJApp-2009-0289-data and writes results to /app/data/results_task_1.txt.

tasks.notes_for_agent: Follow this planned path; do not change the focal claim, dataset, or model family.

tasks.task_instruction: Do not use District, Crop, Distance, Groundwater, or Public goods controls. Do not include Irrigation, credit, and tenancy variables or their interactions. Do not use instruments for water buyer status.

analysis_path.path_description: Same mixed model as Task1 without adding any of the restricted controls; report fixed effects and omnibus test analogs.
preprocessing_steps: Cast village IDs to string for consistent merging.
Select hhcode, village, caste, totinc from household.
Merge domhigh from village onto household by village.
Filter to observations with domhigh in {0,1}.

key_choices.main_predictor_measure: Village dominance indicator (domhigh) and caste categories with interaction.
key_choices.sample_restriction: Keep households in villages with domhigh coded 0 or 1.
key_choices.missing_data_rule: Implicit listwise deletion via filtering domhigh to {0,1}.
key_choices.data_processing: Type casting of keys; left join household to village; restrict to needed variables.
key_choices.model_choice: Linear mixed-effects model with random intercept for village.
key_choices.inference_rule: Two-sided p-values / Wald z tests for fixed effects at alpha=0.05.

main_predictor.name: domhigh (with caste and interaction)
main_predictor.definition: Indicator for high-caste dominated village (1) vs low-caste dominated village (0), interacted with household caste categories.

analysis_code.notes: Python translation mirrors Task1 with additional reporting of Wald z for fixed effects (no direct lmerTest-style ANOVA). Writes to /app/data/results_task_2.txt.

tasks.notes_for_agent: Use the same model and restrictions specified; do not add excluded controls or instruments.

files.data/analysis_task_1__py.py: Loads household.dta and village.dta from /app/data/AEJApp-2009-0289-data, merges on village, filters domhigh to {0,1}, fits MixedLM: totinc ~ C(caste) + domhigh + C(caste):domhigh with random intercept for village, writes summary to /app/data/results_task_1.txt.
files.data/analysis_task_2__py.py: Same data steps as Task1, fits identical MixedLM, additionally reports Wald z for fixed effects, writes summary to /app/data/results_task_2.txt.

codebase.notes: Python translations of R scripts (analysis_task_1.R, analysis_task_2.R) created for execution. Requires statsmodels and pandas. Ensure the AEJApp-2009-0289-data folder is mounted at /app/data/AEJApp-2009-0289-data in the container.

analysis.instructions: Execute Task1 and Task2 using the original Stata datasets (household.dta and village.dta) to fit the specified mixed-effects models and write structured outputs to /app/data.
not_allowed_actions: replace the planned path with a different analysis path

debugging_rule.repair_scope: fix dependency, file path, or output extraction issues only; do not redesign the analysis

analysis.comparison_metrics: Fixed-effect coefficients and p-values (or Wald z) for domhigh main effect and caste-by-domhigh interactions; model summaries; random intercept variance; conclusion text alignment with focal claim.
